pattern: Every node's true value is fully determined by the path taken to reach it (start at 0, go 2x+1 for a left step and 2x+2 for a right step), so recovering a wiped-out tree is just a matter of walking its shape once and recomputing values from the root down.
hints: The tree's shape is given as a level-order array (root first, then children level by level, breadth-first) with null marking a missing child; only the shape is trustworthy, every real value has been wiped to a placeholder.

Recompute values top-down from the shape alone: the root is always 0, a left child's true value is 2 * parent + 1, and a right child's true value is 2 * parent + 2 — but walk the array breadth-first the same way it was written (a missing child consumes its null slot but never reserves array space for grandchildren), don't assume a node's two children sit at fixed arithmetic offsets in the flat array.

Do a single queue-driven breadth-first pass over the array carrying along each node's already-recovered value, computing each child's value only when its slot is not null, and collect every recovered value into a set so find(target) is a plain membership check.

edge_inputs.description: root with only a right child; target must recover the right-child formula
root with only a left child; target must miss the never-computed right value
single contaminated node, only the root value (0) exists
a full three-level tree, querying the deepest recovered value and one just out of reach

a null on the root's left branch shifts every later array slot leftward compared to naive fixed-offset (2i+1/2i+2) indexing on the flat array; a solution that assumes fixed offsets instead of walking the shape breadth-first will miss the grandchild
a lopsided chain of only-left children (root down four levels); nearby values that look plausible but were never on the all-left path must be rejected
querying target 0 must succeed only through the root, and a value that was never assigned on any path must fail

stress.description: random tree shapes of varying size and depth, queried with a mix of recoverable and bogus targets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    def build(depth):
        if depth <= 0 or rng.random() < 0.35:
            return None
        return [build(depth - 1), build(depth - 1)]
    def to_level_order(node):
        out = []
        queue = [node]
        while queue:
            cur = queue.pop(0)
            if cur is None:
                out.append(None)
                continue
            out.append(-1)
            queue.append(cur[0])
            queue.append(cur[1])
        while out and out[-1] is None:
            out.pop()
        return out
    depth = rng.randint(1, 8)
    rooted = [build(depth - 1), build(depth - 1)]
    level_order = to_level_order(rooted)
    queries = max(1, size // 20)
    ops = ['FindElements']
    args = [[level_order]]
    for _ in range(queries):
        ops.append('find')
        args.append([rng.randint(0, 2 ** min(depth + 2, 18))])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: the harness never sees a real TreeNode object here — root travels as a plain level-order array of -1/null placeholders built by the same breadth-first serializer the reference deserializes with, guaranteeing every generated shape is well-formed
